title: Siemens home | Siemens
body: Audi is virtualizing the shop floor with virtual PLC Reducing the variety and number of devices and converting applications to software to make production faster, more robust and more flexible—Audi’s goal was to virtualize shop floor automation. Artificial intelligence Digital twin Digital transformation Infrastructure IT/OT convergence Sustainability Artificial intelligence all Using AI to save water Swedish water company VA SYD brought down its volume of non-revenue water from 10% to less than 8%. How? By using an AI-based solution to detect leaks as small as 0.5 liters per second. This is your front-row seat to the technology and expert insights that are actively engineering a more sustainable and intelligent future. Amberg, Germany all Siemens to invest over €200 million to build a factory A new AI-driven, digitalized factory will be built in Amberg by 2030 to modernize manufacturing and meet rising demand for high-tech electronic products and increase the flexibility of its future manufacturing operations. all Annual Shareholders' Meeting 2026 Siemens has held its Annual Shareholders' Meeting on February 12, 2026 and released our first quarter results for fiscal year 2026. all Siemens Nanjing facility named WEF "Lighthouse" Our digital factory in Nanjing has been named a "Lighthouse" by the World Economic Forum, recognizing its leadership in applying Fourth Industrial Revolution technologies like artificial intelligence and data analytics. Press all Siemens and NVIDIA expand partnership We are expanding our partnership with NVIDIA to build the industrial metaverse. Together, we are reinventing the entire end-to-end industrial value chain. all Siemens & Stadler deliver automated Copenhagen trains A Siemens and Stadler consortium will deliver 226 fully automated (GoA4) trains for S-Bane Copenhagen. This landmark project will create the world's largest automated railway system, boosting capacity for millions of new journeys. all Siemens Mobility leads in EU taxonomy results Siemens Mobility reports its best-ever EU Taxonomy result, with 87% of revenue now Taxonomy-aligned. This externally audited figure confirms our leadership in sustainable mobility.
